prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. The New Zealand government instituted the COVID-19 Response Recognition Award in 2022. This service award aims to acknowledge both individuals and organizations that played a part in New Zealand's frontline efforts in response to the COVID-19 pandemic. The distribution of these accolades is scheduled to commence in late January 2023, with an anticipated total of approximately 80,000 individual honorees. For individuals, the awards will consist of a lapel pin along with a certificate, both of which were conceived and produced by Mayer and Toye, located in Wellington. Individuals who qualify for this recognition include those who dedicated a cumulative minimum of one month of service within the managed isolation and quarantine (MIQ) framework, at the nation's borders, in COVID-19 testing operations, vaccination programs, or contact tracing initiatives, or as medical professionals attending to individuals afflicted with COVID-19. The award designated for organizations, presented as a digital certificate, will be bestowed upon them.
B. The COVID-19 Response Recognition Award is a service award established by the New Zealand government in 2022 to recognise individuals and organisations who contributed to New Zealand’s frontline workforce COVID-19 response. The awards will be distributed from late January 2023, and it is expected that there will be about 80,000 individual recipients.Awards to individuals will take the form of a lapel pin and certificate, designed and manufactured by Mayer and Toye of Wellington. People eligible to receive the award include those who worked for at least a month in total as part of the managed isolation and quarantine (MIQ) system, at the border, in COVID-19 testing, vaccination or contact tracing, or as medical staff caring for COVID-19 patients.The organisational award, in the form of a digital certificate, will
C. In 2022, the New Zealand government introduced the COVID-19 Response Recognition Award, which recognizes organizations and individuals for their contributions to New Zealand's frontline workforce during the COVID-19 response. The awards will be distributed from late January 2023 and will include a lapel pin and certificate. Individuals who have worked in the managed isolation and quarantine system, border, vaccination or contact tracing, or as medical staff caring for COVID-19 patients will be eligible for the award.

Answer:

The award designated for organizations, presented as a digital certificate, will be bestowed upon them.

options.C: In 2022, the New Zealand government introduced the COVID-19 Response Recognition Award, which recognizes organizations and individuals for their contributions to New Zealand's frontline workforce during the COVID-19 response. The awards will be distributed from late January 2023 and will include a lapel pin and certificate.
